methods.description: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) was used to identify and explain the variance in diatom assemblages in Lake Stowell. The first three axes of the PCA were statistically significant and explained 58% of the variance, with associations to salinity, lake water depth, and nutrient concentrations.

steps.description: The first three axes of the PCA were statistically significant and explained 58% of the variance in the diatom assemblages of Lake Stowell.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA to explain variance in diatom assemblages

steps.description: The first diatom PCA axis likely represents an association with salinity; it was positively loaded on marine and brackish taxa and negatively loaded on taxa typical of freshwater lakes or warm conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze PCA axis 1 for salinity association

steps.description: The second PC axis could be related to lake water depth; it was positively loaded on many of the small fragilarioids and larger benthic diatoms, and negatively loaded on planktonic taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze PCA axis 2 for water depth association

steps.description: The third PC axis is likely associated with lake nutrient concentrations; it was positively loaded on taxa with higher optima for nitrogen and/or phosphorus, and negatively loaded on taxa with lower tolerances for nutrient-rich waters.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze PCA axis 3 for nutrient concentration association
